mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate over the preceding 3 months signals monetary easing that reduces the discount rate applied to equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days. The mechanism operates continuously without threshold activation.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 2.27% on 2018-12-01 reflects recent Fed rate cuts from 2.5% in September 2018
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in periods of declining FEDFUNDS are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over a 3-month window, it signals deteriorating labor market conditions that trigger equity risk-off behavior, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days as investors reprrice growth expectations downward.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 3.9% on 2018-12-01 represents historically tight labor market
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with labor market inflection
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 65% of triggered instances despite rising unemployment signal, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A joint contraction in retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) signals weakening aggregate demand that reduces corporate earnings expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days. The mechanism requires both series to decline simultaneously to activate.
candidate_trigger: both RSAFS and HOUST decline > 5% month-over-month
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1256.0K in November 2018 shows recent weakness from prior year levels
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 60% of joint-decline triggered instances, or the correlation between the RSAFS-HOUST interaction term and forward returns is not statistically significant (p > 0.05) over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in industrial production (INDPRO) over 2 months signals supply-side weakness and reduced capacity utilization, which compresses profit margins and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days. The mechanism activates when INDPRO falls below its 6-month moving average.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO falls below its 6-month moving average
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 109.39 in November 2018 shows recent volatility
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns following INDPRO declines below MA are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in payroll employment (PAYEMS) over 3 months signals robust labor demand and consumer income growth, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days through increased corporate revenue expectations. The mechanism operates continuously without threshold activation.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 150263K in December 2018 reflects strong employment growth through 2018
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in periods of rising PAYEMS are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 2.5% and inflation (CPIAUCSL) rises more than 0.2% month-over-month, the Fed is in restrictive monetary policy stance that compresses equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days. The mechanism requires both conditions to hold simultaneously.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 2.27% and CPIAUCSL at 252.73 on 2018-12-01
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with inflation acceleration
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 60% of joint-trigger instances, or the interaction term between FEDFUNDS and CPIAUCSL momentum shows Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in real GDP growth (GDPC1) below 2% annualized rate signals economic slowdown that reduces corporate earnings growth expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days. The mechanism activates when quarterly GDP growth falls below the 2% threshold.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 annualized growth rate falls below 2%
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 18664.97B in Q2 2018 with vintage revision risk noted
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 65% of sub-2% growth triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window following GDP slowdown signals.

mechanism_narrative: A joint acceleration in housing starts (HOUST) and retail sales (RSAFS) over 2 months signals strengthening aggregate demand and consumer confidence, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days as investors reprice growth expectations upward. The mechanism requires both series to accelerate simultaneously.
candidate_trigger: both HOUST and RSAFS increase > 3% month-over-month
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1256K and RSAFS at 513531M in late 2018
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day returns in > 40% of joint-acceleration triggered instances, or the correlation between the HOUST-RSAFS interaction term and forward returns is not statistically significant (p > 0.05) over any 3-year window.
